title: Federal Reserve Board announces it has made the joint findings with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency required for the OCC to approve a request by Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A., for an exemption under section 23A of the Federal Reserve Act
summary: The Federal Reserve Board announced on March 26, 2026 that it has made the joint findings with the OCC required to approve Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A.'s request for a section 23A exemption. The exemption is sought to facilitate an internal corporate reorganization involving the bank's affiliate, Morgan Stanley Europe SE in Frankfurt, Germany. Section 23A establishes limits and requirements on a bank's transactions with its affiliates.
key_points: Federal Reserve Board made joint findings with OCC required for approving Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A.'s section 23A exemption request
The requesting entity is Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A. headquartered in Salt Lake City, Utah
The exemption is for an internal corporate reorganization involving Morgan Stanley Europe SE, Frankfurt am Main, Germany
Section 23A of the Federal Reserve Act establishes limits and imposes requirements on a bank's transactions with affiliates
The announcement includes separate statements by Vice Chair Philip N. Jefferson, Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle W. Bowman, Governor Michael S. Barr, and Governor Lisa D. Cook
why_it_matters: This is a routine regulatory approval for a specific bank's internal reorganization. It has no broader market or macro implications. Section 23A exemptions are relatively common administrative actions that allow banks to restructure affiliate relationships under regulatory supervision. The market impact is negligible.
market_structure_context: The source pertains to section 23A of the Federal Reserve Act, which governs the limits and requirements on bank transactions with affiliates. This is a regulatory framework element that structures how banks can conduct business with related entities, though this specific action is a narrow exemption approval rather than a policy change.
